title: Citrusy Pillowy Ukrainian Monsoon Bisque
description: A unique fusion of Ukrainian and Indian flavors in a refreshing, pillowy citrus bisque featuring peppercorns and a hint of sweetness from a touch of honey.
instructions: In a medium saucepan, melt the butter over medium heat.
Add the flour and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Slowly pour in the cream, whisking constantly to prevent lumps.
Bring the mixture to a simmer and cook for 5 minutes, or until it thickens.
Remove the saucepan from the heat and stir in the orange juice, lemon juice, honey, black peppercorns, and salt.
Let the mixture steep for 10 minutes to allow the flavors to meld.
Strain the mixture through a fine-mesh sieve into a clean bowl, pressing on the solids to extract as much liquid as possible.
Return the mixture to the saucepan and bring it to a simmer over medium heat.
Reduce the heat to low and cook, stirring constantly, for 2 minutes, or until the bisque is heated through.
Taste and adjust the seasoning as needed.
Pour the bisque into fondue pots or individual serving cups.
Garnish with fresh mint leaves and serve immediately.
To make the bisque in a large saucepan, simply multiply the ingredients and cooking time accordingly.
